messages.author: s-ol
messages.text: yes, that looks like a similar approach that i took for my own project. i simplified it down into a single crate for this example, you can see the setup of the radio in src/bin/example/radio.rs and the decoding of the package at the bottom of src/lib.rs: https://git.s-ol.nu/t144-meshcore-example/

messages.author: redengin
messages.content_html: MeshCore = { git = "https://git.housedillon.com/wdillon/Meshcore-rs.git", branch = "s-ol/non-owned", default-features = false }

messages.author: redengin
messages.text: please add 'pubish = false' to https://git.housedillon.com/wdillon/Meshcore-rs/src/branch/main/Cargo.toml#L14 crates.io shouldn't be a namespace for non-reusable code - rather one should only publish if there is a significant number of users (i.e. the crate is useful and will be maintained)
messages.content_html: please add 'pubish = false' to https://git.housedillon.com/wdillon/Meshcore-rs/src/branch/main/Cargo.toml#L14 crates.io shouldn't be a namespace for non-reusable code - rather one should only publish if there is a significant number of users (i.e. the crate is useful and will be maintained)

messages.reply_html:
redengin
when I asked for Meshore no_std support I was told it wouldn't happen - but it appears a branch is supporting no_std This implemenation doesn't appear to be maintained (last commits were 3mo ago) and meshcore has evolved faster

messages.text: this one I'm talking about doesn't have high level tasks right now and if it every will they'd be an optional outer layer

redengin
I also have lots of concerns about how to implement the LoRa tasks for meshcore (i.e. when to Rx, vs when to Tx)

messages.author: s-ol
messages.text: and as you stated that's the part thats probably most in-flux, and likely for the usercode to want to modify so its lower priority

esp. since any reasonably integratable approach will depend on some synchronization/ipc primitives so will be more platform-specific than the packet protocol (whether that be std or embassy)
